obj.html_content:
Advice on family visit



Accommodation

● Hotel on George Street

● cost of family room per night: 2 £ (approx.)

Recommended trips

● a tour of the city centre (starts in Carlton Square)

● a trip by to the old fort

Science Museum

● best day to visit:

● see the exhibition about which opens soon

Food

● Clacton Market:

○ good for food

○ need to have lunch before p.m.

Theatre tickets

● save up to % on ticket prices at bargaintickets.com

Free activities

● Blakewell Gardens:

● Roots Music Festival

● climb Telegraph Hill to see a view of the

added_content.ex_information:
Complete the notes below.
Write
ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER
for each answer.

sentence.content: MAN: Sandra – I seem to remember you had some family visitors staying with you recently.
sentence.content_zh: 男：桑德拉，我记得你最近家里来了一些亲戚做客。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Yeah that’s right. My brother and his family were here a couple of months ago.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：是的，没错。我哥哥一家几个月前来过。

sentence.content: MAN: OK good. Well I wanted to ask your advice – I’ve got my cousin and her family visiting next month and as I don’t have kids I’ve no idea where to take them.
sentence.content_zh: 男：好的，我想向你请教一下建议。下个月我表姐和她家人要来玩，但因为我没有孩子，所以完全不知道该带他们去哪里玩。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Right. What about accommodation? Are they going to stay with you in your flat?
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：明白。那住宿方面呢？他们会住在你家吗？

sentence.content: MAN: No, thankfully – there wouldn’t be room – my cousin wants me to recommend a hotel. Do you know anywhere?
sentence.content_zh: 男：不，还好不用，我家没那么大。我表姐让我推荐一家酒店，你知道有什么合适的吗？

sentence.content: SANDRA: Yes, I do actually. I always recommend people stay at the Kings Hotel.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：有啊，我通常都会推荐国王酒店（Kings Hotel）。

sentence.content: MAN: Where’s that near?
sentence.content_zh: 男：在哪儿附近？

sentence.content: SANDRA: It’s about five minutes’ walk from Murray Station – so nice and central. It’s actually on George Street.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：离默里车站大约步行五分钟——位置很方便，在乔治街上。

sentence.content: MAN: Oh yes I know. I think they’re on quite a tight budget – so how much roughly is it to stay there?
sentence.content_zh: 男：哦，我知道那个地方。不过他们的预算好像不太高，住一晚大概多少钱？

sentence.content: SANDRA: If you book a family room it’s about £125 per night. My brother paid for two double rooms in the end and I think that was around £95 for each room.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：如果你订家庭房的话，大概每晚125英镑左右。我哥哥最后订了两间双人房，每间大概是95英镑左右。

sentence.content: MAN: Oh that’s not too bad.
sentence.content_zh: 男：哦，这个价格还不错。

sentence.content: SANDRA: So how old are your cousin’s kids?
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：那你表姐的孩子多大啦？

sentence.content: MAN: Twelve and nine. So I want to organise some trips while they’re here.
sentence.content_zh: 男：一个十二岁，一个九岁。我想趁他们在的时候安排一些活动。

sentence.content: MAN: I was thinking of doing a bus tour of the city centre – as none of them have been here before.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我原本打算带他们坐观光巴士游览市中心，因为他们都没来过这里。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Those bus tours are quite expensive. I think it’s better to do a walking tour – it gives you a much better feel for the city.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：观光巴士挺贵的。我觉得不如自己步行导览，这样更能感受城市氛围。

sentence.content: SANDRA: There’s one that starts from Carlton Square. It takes a couple of hours and doesn’t cost that much.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：有一个导览从卡尔顿广场出发，大概两个小时，费用也不高。

sentence.content: MAN: Sounds good. I’ll look that up – thanks.
sentence.content_zh: 男：听起来不错，我会查一下的，谢谢。

sentence.content: SANDRA: If the weather’s nice, one thing you could do is visit the old fort. You can get there by boat. The whole trip takes half a day.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：如果天气好，你们可以去旧堡垒参观，坐船过去。整个行程大概半天。

sentence.content: MAN: That’s a really good idea. I’d like to do that myself.
sentence.content_zh: 男：这主意真不错，我自己也想去看看。

sentence.content: MAN: And if the weather’s bad I was thinking they could go to the Science Museum. But maybe they could do that when I’m at work.
sentence.content_zh: 男：如果天气不好，我考虑让他们去科学博物馆。不过可能那天我在上班。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Yeah – don’t forget it’s closed on Mondays.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：嗯，别忘了它周一关门。

sentence.content: MAN: They’re here from Saturday for four nights – so Tuesday would be best, I think.
sentence.content_zh: 男：他们是星期六来的，一共住四晚，应该选周二去最好。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Mmm, and it won’t be so crowded then. Saturdays are terrible.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：嗯，那时候也不会那么拥挤。周六人最多。

sentence.content: SANDRA: I took my kids to the exhibition on old computers there and it was far too crowded. I wanted to go back but it’s finished now.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：我之前带我孩子去看那里展出的老式电脑，结果太挤了。我想再去一次，但展览已经结束了。

sentence.content: MAN: That’s a shame. My cousin’s kids would have enjoyed that.
sentence.content_zh: 男：真可惜，我表姐的孩子肯定会喜欢那个展。

sentence.content: SANDRA: There’s another one starting soon on space which looks really good too.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：很快还有一个关于太空的新展览，看起来也很棒。

sentence.content: MAN: OK, well I’ll mention that to my cousin.
sentence.content_zh: 男：好，我会告诉我表姐的。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Have you thought about where to take them to eat?
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：你有没有想过带他们去哪儿吃饭？

sentence.content: MAN: Well I really like all the food stalls at Clacton Market. My cousin’s vegetarian – I know it’s one of the best places for that kind of food.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我很喜欢克拉克顿市场那边的小吃摊。我表姐是素食者，我知道那儿是吃这类食物最好的地方之一。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Definitely, and there’ll be loads of choice for the kids, too.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：对的，而且孩子们也会有很多选择。

sentence.content: SANDRA: You need to get there quite early though – at the weekend most of the stalls stop serving lunch at 2.30.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：不过你要早点去，周末很多摊位中午14:30就不卖了。

sentence.content: MAN: Good point. It’s all going to need careful planning! My cousin said she’d love to take the kids to a show at the theatre – but tickets are so expensive!
sentence.content_zh: 男：好建议。看来这些事都需要好好计划！我表姐说想带孩子们去看一场剧院演出，但门票实在太贵了！

sentence.content: SANDRA: I know. But you can get some good deals if you book online with bargaintickets.com for the following day – on some seats there’s a 75% discount.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：我知道。不过如果你在网上用 bargaintickets.com 预订第二天的票，有些座位可以有75%的折扣。

sentence.content: MAN: Really?! I must try and get some.
sentence.content_zh: 男：真的吗？！我一定要试试看。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Yeah. There are lots of things you can do for free as well – no need to spend a fortune.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：其实还有很多免费的活动可以参加，不需要花太多钱。

sentence.content: MAN: Like what?
sentence.content_zh: 男：比如什么？

sentence.content: SANDRA: They’re coming next month, right? Well, check and see if it’s the same weekend as the Roots Music Festival in Blakewell Gardens.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：他们不是下个月来嘛？你可以查一下是不是正好赶上布莱克韦尔花园的 Roots 音乐节。

sentence.content: MAN: R double-O-T-S?
sentence.content_zh: 男：R-O-O-T-S？

sentence.content: SANDRA: Yeah. Check it out online. It’s always a family-friendly event and there’s no entry charge.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：对，就是这个名字。你可以上网查一下。这个音乐节一直都很适合家庭参加，而且入场免费。

sentence.content: MAN: That sounds perfect.
sentence.content_zh: 男：听起来太棒了。

sentence.content: SANDRA: And if you’re in Blakewell Gardens – climb Telegraph Hill – you’ll be able to look right down on the port. Everyone’s always really impressed because it’s so huge.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：还有，如果你去了布莱克韦尔花园，记得爬电报山（Telegraph Hill），站在山顶可以看到港口的全景。大家都觉得特别震撼，因为港口真的很大。

sentence.content: MAN: Oh yeah – I’ve been meaning to do that for ages. I’ve heard the view’s amazing.
sentence.content_zh: 男：哦对，我一直想去那儿，听说风景真的很美。

sentence.content: SANDRA: Yeah. It’s really worth the effort.
sentence.content_zh: 桑德拉：真的值得一看。

sentence.content: MAN: Well that’s given me loads of ideas. Thanks so much …
sentence.content_zh: 男：你给了我好多好主意，真是太感谢了……

officialExplain.explain: 结合空格后首字母大写的Hotel，判断本题答案为旅馆名称。空格前一行的小标题Accommodation，在原文中原词重现，后续原文中讲到的旅馆名称为Kings，即本题答案。本题的名称未给出字母拼写，需要结合常见词汇及发音进行填写。

结合题干的cost以及空格前的货币符号£，判断答案为价格数字。£对应pound或pounds，意为“镑，英镑”。原文中family room和per night原词重现，原文的about对应题干空格后的（approx.），approx.是approximately和approximate的缩写形式。相关价格数字为(£) 125，即本题答案。之后原文还提及around £95，但对应房型是双人房间，不符合题干中的family room家庭间。
原文中trips原词重现对应小标题，接下来先讲到bus tour，题干中的city centre原词重现但结合quite expensive可以排除。接下来结合better判断walking tour为更优选择，而且后文讲到much better feel和doesn’t cost that much，也可以印证walking即本题答案，意为“步行，徒步旅行”。
结合上文以及题干空格前的内容，判断答案与旅行方式相关。原文中先出现题干空格后的old fort，接下来的一句中就讲到by boat。boat即本题答案，意为“船”。

结合空前的best day to visit，判断答案为日期时间类信息。原文中Science Museum原词重现，对应小标题。先提及Mondays，但结合closed排除。之后虽然出现Saturday，但讲到Tuesday would be best，而且结合后文讲到的Saturdays are terrible和too crowded可以排除星期六这一信息。因此Tuesday为符合题干关键词的答案，意为“星期二”。
题干的exhibition在上题相关原文中已经出现。先结合原文中的another one判断讲到下一项内容，结合starting soon对应题干空格后的opens soon。原文同一句中讲到on space，on表示“关于”，对应题干空格前的about。space即本题答案，意为“太空，空间”。
原文中Food和Clacton Market原词重现，接下来讲到My cousin’s vegetarian，进一步提及的one of the best places for that kind of food对应题干的good。因此vegetarian即本题答案，做名词表示“素食者”，做形容词表示“素食的”。
结合题干空格前后的内容，判断答案为时间。原文讲到stop serving lunch at，对应题干空格前的have lunch before，相关时间2.30即本题答案。2.30在本题原文中读作two thirty。
结合题干空格后的“%”，判断答案为数字。原文中theatre和tickets都原词重现，对应小标题。之后讲到的折扣相关数字信息为75% discount，题干中已标注百分号，75即本题答案。
结合题干空格前的内容，判断答案为名词性质。在原文中，Roots Music Festival、Blakewell Gardens和Telegraph Hill都原词重现，之后讲到爬上山可以俯瞰 port，后文中题干的view也原词重现。port即本题答案，意为“港口”
